title: Symphony of Evangelion Footage
wikitext:
[]: Symphony of Evangelion Footage             Quote Post by 1001GreatGoals » Mon Nov 25, 2013 4:39 pm I recently purchased the footage and audio from the Symphony of Evangelion, it is on a LaserDisc, and I don't know of any easy way of reading it. What would you guys recommend doing? == Here are some photos of the outside case, I'll take some of the actual disc and such later. http://i.imgur.com/RLPu5Zh.jpg http://i.imgur.com/Vagof9P.jpg http://i.imgur.com/pg1IiCX.jpg == I'd really like to find a way to read the disc and rip the video off.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Nov 25, 2013 4:45 pm Get laserdisc player Get capture device Plug laserdisc player into capture device ????? PROFIT!!!

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Nov 26, 2013 5:39 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Get laserdisc player Get capture device Plug laserdisc player into capture device ????? PROFIT!!!

[]:             Quote Post by 1001GreatGoals » Wed Nov 27, 2013 1:09 am The disc has Digital Audio on it, I'm not sure if it has both Analog and Digital, but I know it does have Digital, it even says on the back at the very bottom in the middle - http://i.imgur.com/pg1IiCX.jpg , it's supposed to be identical to an Audio CD in quality. Rather than buying a player just to rip this one disc, is there anywhere that I could send this too to get a high quality rip? Since LaserDisc's have no sort of error correction, is there any way to get exactly the same information off the disc that is currently on it if it has no scratches or smudges on it? How deep can scratches be on the disc before it interferes with the information stored on it?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Nov 27, 2013 1:46 am The video is analog, so simply reading from the medium introduces noise. There's a lot of shady looking services online that do laserdisc transfers, I've never used any of them. You're probably better off finding someone with a player that you can maybe borrow and buy a capture card. Any of the transfer services will probably be just as expensive.

[]:             Quote Post by ILUVASUKALANGLEY » Sun Dec 01, 2013 8:45 pm I plan on copying this along with other things. but it will be next year. I will be using a LD-S2 Elite player which should be on the high end scale almost top tier like the Hi-Vision Muse players. As for the computer I will be starting from the ground up and should eventually be able to handle everything including Hi-Def. This will take time to get the funds together and do the actual recording. The computer will be using the shell of a CLD-79 Elite LaserDisc player which I had bought for the remote and manual. The player had problems so I decided to use it for a computer case.
